items.note: The Islamabad MoU is signed and sent to Congress, but the Friday Bürgenstock implementation talks were postponed — Vance and Iran's Qalibaf dropping out — because Iran insists the Lebanon fighting stop first. A signed text discovered its first front is run by parties who never signed.

Not a reopening but a permissioned corridor: PGSA requires a permit, 48-hour review and approved insurance, the mined central route stays closed under an estimated 80 mines, and Treasury already designated the authority as an extortion vehicle — so the passage paper may itself be the compliance problem.

The tripwire: the text says "including in Lebanon," but strikes continued through 17–18 June — 18 killed since midnight, four Israeli soldiers killed in a Hezbollah ambush — and Israel, not a party, says the southern zone's 200,000 residents "are not returning."

JPMorgan pulled Claude for Hong Kong staff after Goldman did, turning the Commerce export order into a contract-and-compliance switch. The inference: a frontier API is now a policy endpoint — degradable by state order, vendor licensing or a bank's approved-tool list.

items.what: Russia launched roughly 90 Shahed drones overnight, with ships among the targets — the under-covered Black Sea front grinding on while the Middle East peace text absorbs the headlines and Odesa's grain capacity stays in question.

The reported ~$300bn US-Iran reconstruction fund is conditional, paper capital — pledged inside a process whose first implementation meeting was just postponed. A number that large only becomes real after enforcement and the 60-day clock deliver, not on announcement.

The EU's 2028–34 budget fight is opening with the usual fault lines — net contributors against cohesion and farm spending — a slow structural clash that will outlast every ceasefire timeline now on the calendar.

China is tightening export checks on indium as AI hardware demand rises, adding another chokepoint to the materials side of the compute build-out just as the access side hardens through export controls and bank compliance.

Bankers are preparing investor meetings for a possible SpaceX bond of at least $20bn to fund its AI expansion, after investment-grade ratings from Moody's, Fitch and S&P — debt joining stock as the second gear of the capital machine.
